best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-system-003 title: verify data display interaction module: system source pages: 1 preconditions: user is authenticated and has access to the data display. test data: no specific data required. test steps: 1. user interacts with the data display. 2. data display requests data from the data organizer. 3. data organizer retrieves data from firebase. 4. data display displays the retrieved data. expected result: data display correctly shows the requested data.

test case id: tc-system-007 title: verify message retrieval module: message source pages: 2 preconditions: user is authenticated and has access to a chat. test data: chat identifier. test steps: 1. user requests messages from a chat. 2. system retrieves messages associated with the chat identifier. 3. system displays the retrieved messages to the user. expected result: messages are retrieved and displayed correctly.

test case id: tc-system-001 title: verify user authentication module: system source pages: 1 preconditions: user is registered in the system. test data: valid username and password. test steps: 1. user enters valid username and password. 2. system attempts to authenticate the user. 3. system checks if the credentials match the stored data. 4. system displays a success message if authentication is successful. expected result: user is authenticated successfully and redirected to the main interface.

test case id: tc-system-003 title: verify data display interaction module: system source pages: 1 preconditions: user is authenticated and has access to the data display. test data: no specific data required. test steps: 1. user interacts with the data display. 2. data display requests data from the data organizer. 3. data organizer retrieves data from firebase. 4. data display displays the retrieved data. expected result: data display correctly shows the requested data.

test case id: tc-system-005 title: verify chat creation module: chat source pages: 2 preconditions: user is authenticated. test data: chat identifier and two urls. test steps: 1. user creates a new chat. 2. system generates a unique chat identifier. 3. system stores the chat identifier and urls. 4. system associates the chat with the user. expected result: chat is created successfully and stored in the system.

test case id: tc-system-006 title: verify message sending module: message source pages: 2 preconditions: user is authenticated and has access to a chat. test data: message content, chat identifier, and receiver. test steps: 1. user sends a message. 2. system generates a unique message identifier. 3. system stores the message content, chat identifier, and receiver. 4. system associates the message with the user and chat. expected result: message is sent successfully and stored in the system.

test case id: tc-system-008 title: verify post creation module: post source pages: 2 preconditions: user is authenticated. test data: post content, user nickname, fridge id, type, and cost. test steps: 1. user creates a new post. 2. system generates a unique post identifier. 3. system stores the post content, user nickname, fridge id, type, and cost. 4. system associates the post with the user and fridge. expected result: post is created successfully and stored in the system.

test case id: tc-system-009 title: verify fridge ownership module: fridge source pages: 2 preconditions: user is authenticated. test data: fridge id and user nickname. test steps: 1. user requests ownership of a fridge. 2. system verifies the user's credentials. 3. system associates the fridge with the user. expected result: fridge ownership is verified and associated with the user.

test case id: tc-system-0010 title: verify product removal from fridge module: fridge source pages: 3 preconditions: user is authenticated and owns a fridge. test data: no specific data required. test steps: 1. user initiates the removal of a product from the fridge. 2. schema principale sends a changefragment() message to fragment frigorifero. 3. fragment frigorifero sends a rimuoviprodotto() message to firebase database. 4. firebase database processes the request and removes the product. expected result: product is removed from the fridge and the change is reflected in the firebase database.
